Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Shawn Tillis, Esq. of Winer, McKenna & Burritt LLP, T:510.433.1000, for plaintiff/moving party;
Andre R. Hill, Esq. and T. Connor O'Carroll, Esq. of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum, T:415.397.9006, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was argued. At the end of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF ANNA SMITH'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS is GRANTED IN PART. Per the report of Discovery Pro Tem Judge Dianne Peebles, the parties stipulated that all records responsive to the subpoenas be produced by the custodian of records without redactions except for pages labeled AS144 and AS 348. As to those pages, the court agrees with Ms. Peebles that those pages be redacted as proposed by Ms. Smith. Defendant Taj Campton Palace Hotel's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $1400.

The parties are to submit a stipulated protective order by email today.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Defendant Taj Campton Place Hotels Notice Of Motion And Motion: To Enforce The Court August 8, 2017 Order Requiring Plaintiff To Provide Code Complaint Discovery Responses; And For An Order Imposing Just Sanctions Due To Plaintiffs Disobedience Of The Courts Order
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Shawn Tillis Esg. 510-433-1000 ext. 123 of Winer McKenna & Burritt LLP for Plaintiff/Opposing party through Court Call.
T. Connor O'Carroll, Esq. 415-397-9006 of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum for Defendant/moving party.

Both parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TAJ CAMPTON PLACE HOTEL's Motion To Enforce The Court August 8, 2017 Order Requiring Plaintiff To Provide Code Complaint Discovery Responses; And For An Order Imposing Just Sanctions Due To Plaintiffs Disobedience Of The Courts Order.

Motion denied. The motion to compel was untimely. The parties requests for sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must submit it directly to the Judge Pro Tem at eriles@strikebacklaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth Riles, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)
